steps.title: Track praise versus reach
steps.content: The text contrasts Crowley's limited commercial fame with strong praise from influential champions. Bloom reportedly read Little, Big many times, called himself a lifelong enthusiast, and described the book as a masterwork.

steps.title: Reject unsupported comparisons
steps.content: A invents stylistic similarity to Merrill. B says the masterwork view is not widely held, which the text does not establish. C compares Crowley to Merrill, but the text never ranks them.

steps.title: Infer desired recognition
steps.content: The praise from notable figures suggests that some would want Crowley's work to have more commercial success than it has. The answer is D.
